core_mechanism: separation-of-timescales online update of a persistent latent state, in which the state is re-estimated by gradient updates only intermittently (slow, batched) while being read out and applied every step (fast), with an anchoring penalty and a cached reference that regularize the fast readout against drift away from the slowly-updated state
brief_reason: The abstract explicitly proposes to decouple the frequency of memory updates (periodic, gradient-based) from memory application (per-frame) and adds a Memory Loss that forces persistent internalization plus a Memory Caching regularizer against catastrophic drift, which is a two-timescale adaptive-state scheme with drift-anchoring.
